Object.Name: Timeslot
Object.Description1: Timeslot is an instance of a schedule

Item.Name: start
Item.Description: Intended start time in seconds since UNIX epoch. Could NOT be adjusted, the whole timeslot has to be cancelled and rescheduled instead. GW timestamp of the RUNNING state should be quite close to this one and should accurately describe the actual time of starting the action.

Item.Name: adjusted_stop
Item.Description: Best known stop time (from the device standpoint). Depending on the value of `state` could be either in the past or in the future. Could be changed with `timeslot.adjust_duration()` call or with an `actuator.stop()` call. GW timestamp of the state, immediately following the RUNNING, should be very close to this value.

Item.Name: state
Item.Description: TSS state Values ------ 0: IDLE: No or invalid schedule 1: SCHEDULED: Schedule is active and is waiting to get scheduled 2: WILL_START: Schedule is ready to start, wait for BE decision 3: SKIPPED: Skip schedule 4: UNUSED: LB firmware used this code for SHORTEN_DURATION 5: RUNNING: Schedule is running 6: STOPPED: Schedule stopped 7: ERROR: An error occurred affecting the schedule 8: OVERWRITTEN: Schedule is overwritten because of manual watering 9: DONE: Schedule finished 10: DELETED: Schedule finished 11: REQUESTED: State machine primed for execution

Item.Name: initial_source
Item.Description:
The reason why this timeslot was created, specifically why `start` has exactly this value. Also applies to why `adjusted_stop` has its value in case `adjustment_source` is NONE.
Values ------ 0: NONE: Only relevant for adjustment. 1: PHYSICAL_BUTTON: Relevant for start reason only. 2: ERROR: Relevant for stop reason only, e.g. valve overcurrent. 3: SCHEDULE: For scheduled events. 4: BOOK_KEEPING: Only used internally by the scheduler. 10: BLUETOOTH: Used by Bluetooth application interface. 16: SMART_SYSTEM: Used by smart system app, e.g. for manual start & stop actions. 17: HOMEKIT: Used by HomeKit a.k.a. Apple Home. 18: WEB_SOCKET: Used by websocketd (for integrations like Home Assistant).

Item.Name: adjustment_source
Item.Description: How adjusted_stop was set. Same values as for initial_source.

Item.Name: pre_offset
Item.Description: Magic cookie used by backend. Copied over from schedule config.

Item.Name: adjust_duration
Item.Description: Adjust duration of planned or running timeslot. Only has effect on timeslots in states SCHEDULED, WILL_START, RUNNING (has no effect on timeslots in all other states). Takes two arguments: 0: integer: adjustment source. Updates the adjustment_source field. See initial_source above for list of available values. 1: integer: new duration in seconds. Uses OMA format. Example usage with bnw_cli: $ bnw ipso exc "$DEVICE" timeslot/0/adjust_duration "as=[0='16',1='120']"
